content: FastVideo is a modular framework that separates models (encoders, VAEs, diffusion transformers) from execution logic (pipelines and stages)
Pipelines orchestrate execution by organizing stages such as text encoding, latent preparation, denoising, and decoding
ForwardBatch is the shared state object passed between pipeline stages containing inputs, intermediate states, and outputs
ComposedPipelineBase manages model loading, stage orchestration, and data flow across pipeline stages
Each pipeline stage implements a forward(batch, args) interface and modifies the ForwardBatch state
The denoising stage is the core computation loop that iteratively transforms noise into video frames using diffusion models
FastVideo supports multiple attention backends including FlashAttention, sliding attention, and sparse attention for efficiency
FastVideo uses an executor-worker system where each GPU runs a worker process coordinated by a central executor
FastVideoArgs is the central configuration system controlling model loading, inference settings, and distributed execution
Diffusion transformer models are implemented in fastvideo/models/dits
Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) are implemented in fastvideo/models/vaes
Text and image encoders are implemented in fastvideo/models/encoders
Pipeline implementations are located in fastvideo/pipelines including subdirectories like basic, preprocess, and training
Wan pipelines are implemented in fastvideo/pipelines/basic/wan
Configuration files for models and pipelines are stored in fastvideo/configs
CLI and API entrypoints are implemented in fastvideo/entrypoints including commands like generate and serve
VideoGenerator is the main API for video generation and is implemented in fastvideo/entrypoints/video_generator.py
Use VideoGenerator.from_pretrained() to load pretrained models and initialize pipelines
Basic inference is performed by calling generator.generate_video(prompt, ...) after loading a model
Inference workflow includes loading a model, preparing inputs, running denoising stages, and decoding outputs
To install FastVideo from source, clone the repository and run uv pip install -e .
Recommended setup uses uv virtual environments such as uv venv --python 3.12 and uv pip install fastvideo
FastVideo requires Python 3.10–3.12 and CUDA 11.8+ for GPU execution
Run all tests using pytest tests/ or pytest fastvideo/tests/
Run pre-commit hooks using pre-commit run --all-files before committing changes
To run the Gradio demo, navigate to examples/inference/gradio and run the serving or local demo scripts
Finetuning scripts are located in examples/training/finetune and scripts/finetune
Dataset preparation scripts are located in scripts/dataset_preparation and examples/dataset
FastVideo uses a registry system to dynamically register models, pipelines, and components for extensibility
